source_metadata.title_raw: Patricia Lintell - A-20-023 - February 26, 2020 - Del Rey Oaks

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street ~ Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Patricia Lintell 
City Council of Del Rey Oaks 
860 Rosita Road 
Del Rey Oaks, CA, 93940 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-20-023 
Dear Ms. Lintell: 
February 26, 2020 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
· 
Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for· 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
May you participate in the City's threshold decision, to allow a north side access road 
through the City to the Monterey Regional Airport, where you reside on Rosita Road within 500 to 
1,000 feet of "Option 5" for locating such an access as an extension to your road? 
CONCLUSION 
No. You have a financial interest in your residence because you transferred ownership in the 
real property to your son with a provision giving you title in the event he predeceases you. It is 
reasonably foreseeable that a decision on the Rosita Road, "Option 5," road access will have a 
material financial effect on your financial interest under Regulation 18702.2(a)(8) as it is 
anticipated to substantially alter traffic on your residential road. Therefore, you may not participate 
in the City's General Plan poficy decision on whether to allow any north access road. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Di vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER2 
File No. A-20-023 
Page No. 2 
The City of Del Rey Oaks ("City") is adjacent to the Monterey Regional Airport ("Airport") 
and lies on its northeast side. The City's General Plan has a specific circulation element policy 
prohibition stating that the City will not support the potential north side access from Highway 218 
and Del Rey Gardens Drive or any other airport access through the City for purposes of emergency 
access and access to and from other land uses suqject to the approved Airport Master Plan. This 
prohibition has been in place for approximately 15 to 20 years. The City has a population of 1,650 
and is primarily a residential community. 
In November 2018, the Airport District approved a new Airport Master Plan. This new plan 
calls for development of the Airport's northeast side and includes a proposed north side access road 
to allow general public access to the north side of the Airport. The Airport District has considered 
seven north access road options, at least four of which would run through the small City. The 
Airport District has identified Del Gardens Drive, "Option 3," as the preferred north side access 
point and will carry this as the recommended location moving forward. 3 The Airport Master Plan 
notes this preference came after extensive review and input from Caltrans and other agencies. The 
Airport Master Plan identifies "Option 5" as an extension of Rosita Road; however, it notes the 
extension would introduce airport traffic through a residential neighborhood where a road does not 
currently exist, and other issues: 
This alternative considers utilizing the existing Rosita Road that extends from an 
intersection with Highway 218. A 200-foot extension of this road would be 
needed to reach Airport property: A storm water detention pond is located in the 
path of such a road and would likely need to be reengineered. The detention pond . 
may also be an environmental constraint. The east side of Rosita Road is lined 
with homes. 
The Airport District requests that the City amend its the General Plan policy to allow a 
potential north side access. Currently, the City is considering an amendment to the policy. 
You reside on Rosita Road. You transferred title to this residence to your adult son several 
years ago, with the provision that title reverts to you if he predeceas~s you and you must reimburse 
his surviving spouse for any monies they have invested in the property. You do not pay rent. Your 
residence is within 500 to 1,000 feet from the proposed extension of Rosita Road for access to the 
Airport. You state the following regarding the extension of Rosita Road for the Airport access: 
This would have the effect of a severe increase of traffic in front of my home as 
well as the others who live on Rosita Road. Rosita Road is one of two access 
2 Your request is related to the Monterey Regional Airport Master Plan north side access road issue previously 
submitted by the City attorney on behalf of another councilmember. (Lorca Advice letter, No. A-20-016.) The City 
attorney recommended that you request advice on this issue as well. We rely on the underlying facts from the previous 
letter for this analysis. 
3 See AIRPORT MASTER PLAN-Monterey Regional Airport, Final, pages 5-28-29, Exhibit %T, and pages 6-
20-22, llll ://montere .ainorl tudv.com/files/2020/0 I/MRY-Volume-1-Final-0 12420-r PartS.pclf, as viewed February 
4, 2020. 


File No. A-20-023 
Page No. 3 
roads (the main one) to the South side of Del Rey Oaks off of Highway 218 
(Canyon Del Rey). Rosita Road is heavily trafficked and any increase would in 
my opinion cause a great hazard for safety. In addition, the intersection of Rosita 
Road and Highway 218 is extremely difficult to exit - making a right tum is 
difficult and making a left tum such a great hazard due to the speed and number 
of vehicles traveling on Highway 218. Highway 218 is the route from 
Highway 68 to Highway 1. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit any public official from making, 
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which the official has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) A public official has a 
"financial interest" in a governmental decision,_ within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on 
the public generally, on one or more of the public official's interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 
18700(a).) Section 87103 defines a financial interest to include any real property in which the 
public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. You have identified that you 
have a direct ownership interest in the real property, because of the contract provision that allows 
title to revert to you in the event you survive your son. (Section 82033.) 
Where the financial interest is not explicitly involved in a decision, the financial effect is 
reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility, more than hypothetical or 
theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).) Your real property interest is within the City's General Plan; 
however, it is not the subject of the policy amendment decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) Under 
Regulation 18701 (b ), you will have a financial interest in the decision if it is a realistic possibility 
the decisions will have a material financial effect on your real property interest. 
Pertinent to the facts provided, the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a governmental 
decision on a parcel of real property in which an official has a financial ownership interest is 
material whenever the governmental decision involves property located more than 500 feet but less 
than 1,000 feet from the property line of the parcel, and the decision would change the parcel's 
character by substantially altering traffic levels, 1.ntensity of use, parking, view, privacy, noise 
levels, or air quality; or its market value. (Regulation 18702.2(a)(8).) 
The decision at issue is whether to keep or end the City's policy prohibiting a north side 
access road to the Airport. Ending the policy would potentially allow access to the Airport through 
the City. The Airport District's Option 5 for such a road would extend the residential road on which 
you reside to allow access to the Airport. It is reasonably foreseeable that allowing any north side 
access road through the City may result in an access to the Airport constructed on your residential 
road. You understand that this extension and access would cause in a severe traffic increase in front 
of your home. Therefore, it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision to allow any north side 
access road would have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect on your real property 


File No. A-20-023 
Page No. 4 
interest to the extent Option 5 is still under consideration as a potential access.4 Accordingly, you 
have a disqualifying financial interest under the Act. 
Public Generally 
A public official that has a reasonably foreseeable and material financial interest in a 
decision-may still participate if the official demonstrates that the financial effect is (1) not unique to 
the official and (2) shared by a significant segment of the jurisdiction.5 (Section 87103, Regulation 
18703.) Relevant to these facts, Regulation 18703 ·defines a unique effect on the official's financial 
interest(s) to include a disproportionate effect on either of the following: 
(1) The development potential or use of the official's real property or on the 
income producing potential of the official's real property or business entity. 
(2) An official's business entity or real property resulting from the proximity of a 
project that is the subject of a decision. 
Given that there are several potential options for an access road through the small City of 
1,650 residents, the decision to allow any north access road through the City may impact additional 
residential parcels. However, it appears that your residential real property interest will be uniquely 
affected due to its proximity to Option 5, which may be considered if the City policy is amended. 
Due to this unique effect, the public generally exception is not applicable here. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
LKH:aja 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
/. I!-,~, 
By: 
L. Karen Harrison 
Senior Counsel, L_egal Division 
4 In addition to the materiality standard in Regulation l 8702.2(a)(8), we note a finding of materiality may be 
also be appropriate in the·event ofa "Rosita Road" access decision under Regulation 18702.2(a)(6), involving the 
construction or improvement of streets, if the parcel will receive new or improved services that provide a benefit or 
detriment disproportionate to other properties receiving the services. 
5 A significant segment of the public includes at least 25 percent of all real property or residential real 
property; or all individuals within the official's jurisdiction. (Regulation l 8703(b ).)
